answer: Lục bát is a Vietnamese poetic meter where each couplet consists of two lines: the first line has 6 syllables and the second line has 8 syllables. The meter follows specific tonal rules: the 6th syllable of the first line must have the opposite tone type (flat vs. sharp) from the 6th syllable of the second line, and the 8th syllable of the second line must have the same tone type as the 6th syllable of the first line. This creates a harmonious alternating pattern of flat and sharp tones at key positions that gives the meter its distinctive rhythmic and tonal quality.
